The article titled "Inter-turn Stator Winding Short Circuit Fault Analysis in Inverter fed Induction Motor using FEM" focuses on the analysis of inter-turn stator winding short circuit faults in an inverter-fed induction motor using Finite Element Method (FEM). The authors, M. Karthic Krishna, K. Kishan Kumar, M. Koushik, K. Sudhakaran, and N. Praveen Kumar, presented this work at the 2018 3rd IEEE International Conference on Recent Trends in Electronics, Information & Communication Technology (RTEICT). The article likely discusses the modeling, simulations, and analysis of inter-turn short circuit faults in the stator winding of an induction motor to understand the fault effects and evaluate the motor performance under such conditions.

The article titled "Online detection and diagnosis of short-circuit fault in three-phase inverter-fed induction motor drives using high-frequency voltage injection" focuses on the development of an online detection and diagnosis method for short-circuit faults in three-phase inverter-fed induction motor drives. The authors, G. Lee, M. Jang, and B. W. Lee, propose a technique that utilizes high-frequency voltage injection to detect and diagnose short-circuit faults in real-time. The article likely discusses the theoretical background, methodology, and experimental results related to this approach and its effectiveness in identifying and diagnosing short-circuit faults in inverter-fed induction motor drives.

The article focuses on the development and implementation of fault-tolerant control strategies for three-phase inverter-fed induction motor drives. Specifically, it addresses both open-circuit and short-circuit faults and proposes control methods to maintain motor operation and performance even in the presence of these faults. The paper likely discusses the theoretical background, control algorithm design, simulation or experimental results, and evaluation of the proposed fault-tolerant control techniques.

The article focuses on the development of an online detection method for open-circuit faults in three-phase inverter-fed induction motors using deep belief networks (DBNs). The authors propose utilizing the capabilities of DBNs in analyzing motor current and voltage data to detect open-circuit faults in real-time. The paper likely discusses the methodology, implementation, experimental setup, and evaluation of the proposed approach for online open-circuit fault detection in inverter-fed induction motors.
